issue_title: Improvement Parser.addini - type parameter
context: In pytest, this issue is about Improvement Parser.addini - type parameter.
what_happened: Pytest's Parser.addini() API only handled a limited set of built-in types, so users who wanted integer or float ini values had to fetch strings and convert them manually.
what_should_happen: Pytest should support int and float ini types directly so numeric configuration values are parsed and returned in their proper types.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that Parser.addini() accepts int and float types and returns parsed numeric values instead of raw strings.
pr_title: Improvement for Parser.addini to facilite parsing of 'int' and 'float' values

issue_title: [Feature]: Using other LLM providers than Openrouter; using LLM providers' OAuth instead of API key
context: In Hermes, this issue is about Using other LLM providers than Openrouter; using LLM providers' OAuth instead of API key.
what_happened: The original issue asked for broader provider support and OAuth-based authentication, but the generated test for this item is actually tied to a different fix in the same merged PR.
what_should_happen: For the code under test, repository-level .hermes.md or HERMES.md files should be discovered from the project root, cleaned up correctly, and included in prompt context.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify the .hermes.md project-configuration behavior: locating the repo root, finding the config file, stripping YAML frontmatter, and injecting the file contents into context building.
pr_title: fix+feat: bug fixes, auto session titles, .hermes.md project config
manual_note: The generated test is misaligned with the original issue title and appears to target a different merged fix.

issue_title: Feature: Interactive Session Picker for hermes --continue — Browsable Session List with Search
context: In Hermes, this issue is about Interactive Session Picker for hermes --continue — Browsable Session List with Search.
what_happened: The new session-browsing flow could fail before launching because one branch of cmd_sessions() imported os locally. That made Python treat os as a local name for the entire function, so the browse path could raise UnboundLocalError when it later called os.execvp().
what_should_happen: The session browser should start normally, letting users browse and resume sessions without crashing because of the local os import.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that the session-browsing path no longer raises UnboundLocalError and that the browse command can reach the os.execvp() call successfully.
pr_title: feat: interactive session browser with search filtering (#718)

issue_title: fixtures: change `FixtureDef.cached_result[2]` from exception triplet to exception
context: In pytest, this issue is about fixtures: change FixtureDef.cached_result[2] from exception triplet to exception.
what_happened: I propose changing the type of the 3rd item of FixtureDef.cached_result (which indicates a cached exception outcome of the fixture) from a sys.exc_info style triplet to just the exception instance.
what_should_happen: These are some relatively small changes I made while going over the fixture code.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that the cached fixture error state now uses the simplified exception representation.
pr_title: fixtures: some tweaks & improvements

issue_title: Internal _flush_sentinel marker leaks to external API providers
context: In Hermes, this issue is about Internal _flush_sentinel marker leaks to external API providers.
what_happened: An internal _flush_sentinel marker used during memory flushing could leak into outbound API messages, exposing internal bookkeeping data to external model providers.
what_should_happen: Internal flush metadata should be stripped before messages are sent to an external API.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that _flush_sentinel is removed from outbound API messages during the flush path.
pr_title: fix(agent): strip _flush_sentinel from API messages

issue_title: Bug in _pytest.compat.getfuncargnames() corrupts fixture handling for test methods w/ positional-only self parameter
context: In pytest, this issue is about Bug in _pytest.compat.getfuncargnames() corrupts fixture handling for test methods w/ positional-only self parameter.
what_happened: [x] a detailed description of the bug or problem you are having _Doesn't depend on specific packages_ [ ] output of pip list from the virtual environment you are using _Isn't related to specific Pytest versions_ [ ] pytest and operating system versions [x] minimal example if possible Basic example Passing: python import pytest @pytest.fixture def value_fixture(): return 'value' class TestClass: def test_value(self, value_fixture): assert value_fixture == 'value' Failing: python import pytest @pytest.fixture def value_fixture(): return 'value' class TestClass: def test_value(self, /, value_fixture): assert value_fixture == 'value' Result: python pyfuncitem = <Function test_value> @hookimpl(tr.
what_should_happen: – by supporting positional-only self parameters in _pytest.compat.getfuncargnames() Fixes #13376 _Tests & changelog entry will be added if PR is seen as useful.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify both the low-level getfuncargnames() behavior and end-to-end pytest collection/execution for methods that use positional-only self.
pr_title: Fix parameter handling of non-static test methods

issue_title: [Python 3.13.0b3] Address a deprecated call in `testing/python/fixtures.py::test_getfuncargnames_partial`
context: In pytest, this issue is about Address a deprecated call in testing/python/fixtures.py::test_getfuncargnames_partial.
what_happened: On Python 3.13, one of pytest's own fixture tests started hitting a FutureWarning because functools.partial was being accessed as an instance attribute in a way that changed under newer Python behavior.
what_should_happen: That self-test should be guarded so pytest's test suite handles the Python 3.13 functools.partial behavior cleanly.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that the test_getfuncargnames_partial case is handled correctly on Python 3.13 without surfacing the warning-driven failure path.
pr_title: [pre-commit.ci] pre-commit autoupdate

issue_title: Add `addDuration` to `TestCaseFunction`
context: In pytest, this issue is about Add addDuration to TestCaseFunction.
what_happened: Cpython 3.12 (alpha) has added an addDuration API to test results (python/cpython#12271).
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that TestCaseFunction interoperates correctly with test results that expose the new addDuration API.
pr_title: Python 3.12 alpha fixes

issue_title: pre-commit failing on mypy hook with at least a 'Module has no attribute "getuid" error'
context: In pytest, this issue is about pre-commit failing on mypy hook with at least a 'Module has no attribute "getuid" error'.
what_happened: Description of problem On VS Code, I am attempting to commit a staged change of src\_pytest\config\init.py; however, the pre-commit hook fails on mypy with command ouput: mypy.....................................................................Failed hook id: mypy exit code: 1 src\_pytest\compat.py:324: error: Module has no attribute "getuid"; maybe "getpid" or "getppid"?
what_should_happen: On Windows 10.0.19045, the mypy hook is failing on pre-commit because of two [attr-defined] errors, which prevents me from staging changes.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify the behavior related to pre-commit failing on mypy hook with at least a 'Module has no attribute "getuid" error' in src/_pytest/compat.py, testing/test_parseopt.py.
pr_title: fix: closes #11343's [attr-defined] type errors

issue_title: Please make verbose XFAIL tracebacks optional
context: In pytest, this issue is about Please make verbose XFAIL tracebacks optional.
what_happened: Verbose expected-failure reporting could print full XFAIL tracebacks by default, creating a large amount of output for projects with many expected failures.
what_should_happen: XFAIL tracebacks should remain hidden unless the user explicitly enables them with the dedicated option.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that XFAIL tracebacks stay hidden by default and only appear when --xfail-tb is enabled.
pr_title: implement #12231 new flag --xfail-tb

issue_title: Context compression silently drops history due to Responses API / stream kwarg mismatch in auxiliary client
context: In Hermes, this issue is about Context compression silently drops history due to Responses API / stream kwarg mismatch in auxiliary client.
what_happened: ContextCompressor used get_text_auxiliary_client() and called client.chat.completions.create() directly. When the auxiliary client resolved to a Codex/Responses API backend, this failed with a kwarg error, causing _generate_summary() to return None and silently drop conversation history.
what_should_happen: ContextCompressor now uses call_llm() from au.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that contextCompressor now uses call_llm() from au.
pr_title: feat: centralized provider router, call_llm API, unified /model command

issue_title: Parent class marked fixture no longer reached with pytest 7.2
context: In pytest, this issue is about Parent class marked fixture no longer reached with pytest 7.2.
what_happened: Starting with the release of pytest 7.2, the distutils tests started failing (https://github.com/pypa/distutils/issues/186).
what_should_happen: Base-class fixture markers should still be respected and applied before subclass markers when pytest builds the effective fixture order.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that parent class usefixtures markers are applied before subclass usefixtures markers, ensuring base class fixtures run first.
pr_title: fix #10447 - consider marks in reverse mro order to give base classes priority

issue_title: Installation issue - cannot move ... to '/root/.hermes/node'
context: In Hermes, this issue is about Installation issue - cannot move ... to '/root/.hermes/node'.
what_happened: Install_node() calls mv extracted_dir $HERMES_HOME/node without first ensuring $HERMES_HOME exists, causing mv to fail when ~/.hermes doesn't exist yet.
what_should_happen: The fixed code should add mkdir -p "$HERMES_HOME" before the mv call in install_node().
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that add mkdir -p "$HERMES_HOME" before the mv call in install_node().
pr_title: fix(install): create ~/.hermes before moving Node.js directory

issue_title: [Bug]: hermes gateway systemd unit omits nvm Node path and crash-loops with WhatsApp enabled
context: In Hermes, this issue is about hermes gateway systemd unit omits nvm Node path and crash-loops with WhatsApp enabled.
what_happened: Bug description When Hermes is installed on Linux with Node.js available only through nvm, hermes gateway install writes a systemd user unit whose PATH does not include the resolved Node binary directory.
what_should_happen: The fixed code should include the directory of the resolved node binary when generating the gateway systemd unit PATH keep the existing venv and node_modules/.bin entries unchanged add a regression test covering Node installed under ~/.nvm/.../bin Problem On Linux, if Node is installed via nvm, hermes gateway install can generate a systemd unit whose PATH omits the actual Node binary directory.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify the behavior related to [Bug]: hermes gateway systemd unit omits nvm Node path and crash-loops with WhatsApp enabled in hermes_cli/gateway.py, tests/hermes_cli/test_gateway_service.py.
pr_title: fix(gateway): include resolved node path in systemd unit

issue_title: [Feature]: custom context window limits per model
context: In Hermes, this issue is about custom context window limits per model.
what_happened: The original issue asks for custom context-window limits per model, but the generated test is narrower: it targets local-server context-window detection and related fallback logic.
what_should_happen: For the code under test, Hermes should query supported local runtimes and use the actual available context-window size instead of relying on a single generic fallback value.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify local-server context-window detection and the related recovery logic for missing or incomplete model metadata.
pr_title: feat: query local servers for actual context window size

issue_title: `--force` flag bypasses dangerous verdict in `should_allow_install()`, allowing installation of skills with critical security findings
context: In Hermes, this issue is about --force flag bypasses dangerous verdict in should_allow_install(), allowing installation of skills with critical security findings.
what_happened: The --force flag could incorrectly override a dangerous scan verdict and allow installation of a skill that policy intended to block.
what_should_happen: A dangerous scan verdict should always block installation, even when --force is provided.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that dangerous skill installs remain blocked and that --force only relaxes less severe caution cases.
pr_title: fix: prevent --force from overriding dangerous verdict in should_allow_install

issue_title: doc: TerminalReporter is not documented
context: In pytest, this issue is about TerminalReporter is not documented.
what_happened: Pytest's TerminalReporter was treated as public API in practice, but it was missing from the reference documentation and was not exposed clearly through the public namespace.
what_should_happen: TerminalReporter should be documented as part of pytest's public reference API and be reachable through the expected public import path.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that pytest.TerminalReporter is exposed as public API and documented accordingly.
pr_title: Include TerminalReporter in reference docs #6649

issue_title: Nous provider: resolved agent key not included in inference request auth header
context: In Hermes, this issue is about Nous provider: resolved agent key not included in inference request auth header.
what_happened: When agent_key is missing from auth state, the fallback chain included state.get("access_token"), causing an OAuth token (not a valid inference credential) to be used as the API key. This resulted in 404 responses from the Nous inference API.
what_should_happen: The fixed code should remove state.get("access_token") from the fallback chain so that when agent_key is missing, api_key is empty, which cor.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that remove state.get("access_token") from the fallback chain so that when agent_key is missing, api_key is empty, which cor.
pr_title: fix(nous): don't use OAuth access_token as inference API key

issue_title: bug: post-compression file-read history injected as role=user, breaking message semantics
context: In Hermes, this issue is about post-compression file-read history injected as role=user, breaking message semantics.
what_happened: After context compression, a [Files already read in this session — do NOT re-read these] message is injected into the conversation with role="user".
what_should_happen: Removes the [Files already read — do NOT re-read these] user message injected after context compression.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that the fixed code does NOT inject any such message after compression.
pr_title: fix: remove post-compression file-read history injection

issue_title: pytest.approx Raises misleading error for Sequences/Collections containing different strings
context: In pytest, this issue is about pytest.approx Raises misleading error for Sequences/Collections containing different strings.
what_happened: When pytest.approx was used inside a sequence or collection that also contained strings, pytest could try to perform numeric tolerance math on the string values and produce a misleading error.
what_should_happen: Pytest.approx should apply tolerance logic only to numeric values and leave non-numeric values such as strings to normal equality comparison.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that mixed collections containing strings no longer trigger the misleading numeric-comparison failure path in pytest.approx.
pr_title: Fix 13010 - Ensure pytest.approx in sequence/collections only process instances of Number

issue_title: <think> blocks leak into user-facing responses
context: In Hermes, this issue is about <think> blocks leak into user-facing responses.
what_happened: When a model includes <think>...</think> reasoning blocks in its response, the raw XML tags are displayed to the end user instead of being stripped.
what_should_happen: Fixes #149 The _strip_think_blocks() method existed but was not being called on the final_response in the normal completion path.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify the behavior related to <think> blocks leak into user-facing responses in run_agent.py.
pr_title: fix: strip <think> blocks from final response to users

issue_title: MockAwareDocTestFinder._find_lineno is stale
context: In pytest, this issue is about MockAwareDocTestFinder._find_lineno is stale.
what_happened: In doctest, the comment for _find_lineno is stale: https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/blob/043ff9abc657124db3504d3604e16ddebfe6e28f/src/_pytest/doctest.py#L514-L520 issue17446 resolves to python/cpython#61648, the fix which was released in Python 3.11.
what_should_happen: There was a regression caused by #12431 that excluded the patch on broken CPython patch version ranges 3.11.0–3.11.8 and 3.12.0–3.12.2.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify the behavior related to MockAwareDocTestFinder._find_lineno is stale in src/_pytest/doctest.py.
pr_title: 🚑 Clarify patch condition for doctest finder hack

issue_title: Running tests inside namespace packages ?
context: In pytest, this issue is about Running tests inside namespace packages ?
what_happened: I am having import issues with namespaces packages and pytest.
what_should_happen: Previously, when running --pyargs pkg, if you didn't have pkg/init.py, pytest would fail with ERROR: module or package not found: pkg (missing init.py?).
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify the behavior related to Running tests inside namespace packages ? in changelog/478.feature.rst, doc/en/reference/reference.rst, src/_pytest/main.py.
pr_title: Support PEP420 (implicit namespace packages) as --pyargs target.

issue_title: [Bug]: CLI blocks during tool usage and only resumes when typing
context: In Hermes, this issue is about CLI blocks during tool usage and only resumes when typing.
what_happened: During tool usage on macOS, the CLI could appear frozen until the user typed a key because pending output was not being flushed while the interrupt loop was idle.
what_should_happen: The agent loop should keep invalidating and refreshing the terminal output even when no keyboard input arrives, so tool activity remains visible.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that the CLI no longer stalls visually during tool usage and that the idle loop keeps the prompt-toolkit renderer moving.
pr_title: fix(cli): flush stdout during agent loop to prevent macOS display freeze (#1624)

issue_title: Weird collection stats when filtering from `pytest_collection_modifyitems` hook
context: In pytest, this issue is about Weird collection stats when filtering from pytest_collection_modifyitems hook.
what_happened: Users filtering items inside pytest_collection_modifyitems could end up with confusing collected, selected, and deselected statistics if they did not also call pytest_deselected correctly.
what_should_happen: The hook documentation and behavior should make it clear how deselection is reported so collection stats stay accurate when items are filtered.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify the documented and functional behavior around calling pytest_deselected from pytest_collection_modifyitems.
pr_title: docs: clarify pytest_deselected/pytest_collection_modifyitems usage

issue_title: [Python 3.12] Assertion rewriting triggers DeprecationWarning
context: In pytest, this issue is about Assertion rewriting triggers DeprecationWarning.
what_happened: Pytest's assertion rewriting still relied on deprecated AST node classes, which started raising DeprecationWarning on Python 3.12.
what_should_happen: Assertion rewriting should avoid deprecated AST node types so normal test collection and rewriting do not emit Python 3.12 deprecation warnings.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that assertion rewriting no longer uses the deprecated AST node classes that trigger warnings on Python 3.12.
pr_title: Python 3.12 support

issue_title: Mention CI and BUILD_NUMBER in `pytest -h`
context: In pytest, this issue is about Mention CI and BUILD_NUMBER in pytest -h.
what_happened: The pytest -h output and related documentation did not mention the CI and BUILD_NUMBER environment variables, even though users expected them to be listed alongside other relevant settings.
what_should_happen: Both the help output and the documentation should mention CI and BUILD_NUMBER where pytest explains its environment-variable behavior.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that CI and BUILD_NUMBER appear in the relevant help and documentation surfaces.
pr_title: details impact of specifying CI or BUILD_NUMBER

issue_title: Gateway transcript loses 1 message per turn due to session_meta offset mismatch
context: In Hermes, this issue is about Gateway transcript loses 1 message per turn due to session_meta offset mismatch.
what_happened: GatewayRunner._handle_message() loses messages from the transcript on every conversation turn after the first.
what_should_happen: _run_agent returns history_offset = len(agent_history) (filtered) The caller uses history_offset instead of len(history) for the slice The else branch returns [] instead of all agent_messages These te.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that _run_agent returns history_offset = len(agent_history) (filtered) The caller uses history_offset instead of len(history) for the slice The else branch returns [] instead of all agent_messages These te.
pr_title: fix(gateway): use filtered history length for transcript message extraction

issue_title: [Bug]: Discord gateway silently drops valid document attachments due to restrictive size and extension handling
context: In Hermes, this issue is about Discord gateway silently drops valid document attachments due to restrictive size and extension handling.
what_happened: The Discord gateway rejected valid diagnostic attachments in two common cases: .log files were treated as unsupported, and ZIP files above 20 MB were skipped even when Discord itself allowed the upload.
what_should_happen: The Discord integration should accept plain .log files and should allow ZIP attachments up to the size limit that Discord actually supports for this workflow.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that .log attachments are accepted and that ZIP files in the supported size range are no longer dropped by the Discord document-ingestion path.
pr_title: fix(discord): accept .log attachments and raise document size limit

issue_title: [Bug]: NameError: name 'subprocess' is not defined
context: In Hermes, this issue is about NameError: name 'subprocess' is not defined.
what_happened: The NeuTTS dependency installer called subprocess.run() and referenced subprocess exceptions without importing the subprocess module, which caused a NameError during setup.
what_should_happen: The setup path should import subprocess before using it so NeuTTS dependency installation can run normally.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that _install_neutts_deps() no longer raises NameError because of a missing subprocess import.
pr_title: fix+feat: bug fixes, auto session titles, .hermes.md project config

issue_title: [Bug]: 钉钉无法连接。
context: In Hermes, this issue is about 钉钉无法连接。.
what_happened: The issue reports that the DingTalk integration could not connect after a fresh install, likely because required runtime pieces were missing from the installed dependencies.
what_should_happen: For the code under test, the relevant tool definitions and handler wiring should be registered correctly so the affected runtime path can start without missing imports or mismatched tool names.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify the concrete runtime changes covered by the generated test: updated tool names, the added terminal tool registration, the correct API-key environment variable, and the missing fire import.
pr_title: Terminal tool

issue_title: __tracebackhide__ does not work with chained exceptions
context: In pytest, this issue is about __tracebackhide__ does not work with chained exceptions.
what_happened: When one exception was raised while handling another, __tracebackhide__ markers on the hidden helper frames were not respected, so chained exceptions exposed traceback entries that users expected pytest to hide.
what_should_happen: Hidden traceback frames should remain hidden even when pytest renders a chained exception.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that __tracebackhide__ still suppresses the marked frames in chained-exception tracebacks.
pr_title: Fix hidden traceback entries of chained exceptions getting shown

issue_title: WhatsApp DM interrupt silently fails due to session key mismatch in _quick_key
context: In Hermes, this issue is about WhatsApp DM interrupt silently fails due to session key mismatch in _quick_key.
what_happened: When a WhatsApp DM user sends a new message while the agent is still processing a previous one, the interrupt mechanism silently fails.
what_should_happen: The _quick_key used for interrupt lookup and _pending_messages in _handle_message() omitted chat_id for all DMs.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify the behavior related to WhatsApp DM interrupt silently fails due to session key mismatch in _quick_key in gateway/run.py, tests/gateway/test_session.py.
pr_title: fix(gateway): match _quick_key to _generate_session_key for WhatsApp DMs

issue_title: Context overrun
context: In Hermes, this issue is about Context overrun.
what_happened: When a local inference backend hit its context limit, Hermes could surface the backend error directly instead of recovering cleanly from the context-overrun condition.
what_should_happen: Hermes should detect the context-overrun response from supported local backends and recover gracefully instead of crashing or leaving the session in a broken state.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify the context-overrun recovery path for local inference backends.
pr_title: Fix context overrun crash with local LLM backends

issue_title: Slow collection time when tests are not in a relative folder to the current working folder
context: In pytest, this issue is about Slow collection time when tests are not in a relative folder to the current working folder.
what_happened: Collection could become unnecessarily slow when tests were located outside a path relative to the current working directory because the same initial-path relpath checks were repeated many times.
what_should_happen: Pytest should cache those initial path checks so collection remains fast even for deeply nested test trees outside the current working directory.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify the cached _check_initialpaths_for_relpath behavior and the updated helper signature used by the fix.
pr_title: Fix: #13420 - Add cache for nodes._check_initialpaths_for_relpath

issue_title: [Feature]: Kimi Code and Codex Spark support
context: In Hermes, this issue is about Kimi Code and Codex Spark support.
what_happened: The issue title is about Kimi Code and Codex Spark support, but the generated test is actually reusing the Hermes project-configuration fix from the same batch PR.
what_should_happen: For the code under test, .hermes.md or HERMES.md files should be found from the repo root and their contents should be usable as project-level context.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify the .hermes.md project-config behavior: repo-root lookup, config-file lookup, frontmatter stripping, and prompt-context injection.
pr_title: fix+feat: bug fixes, auto session titles, .hermes.md project config
manual_note: The generated test is misaligned with the issue and seems to target a reused `.hermes.md` fix.

issue_title: mcp_bluebubbles_send_imessage failing with empty error message
context: In Hermes, this issue is about mcp_bluebubbles_send_imessage failing with empty error message.
what_happened: Sending an iMessage through the BlueBubbles integration could fail with an empty or unhelpful error, making the new gateway path hard to diagnose.
what_should_happen: The BlueBubbles platform support should be wired in cleanly so configuration, platform registration, channel routing, and send behavior all exist for the iMessage gateway.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify the concrete BlueBubbles integration points covered by the generated test: platform enum support, config loading, toolset registration, and send-path routing.
pr_title: feat(gateway): add BlueBubbles iMessage platform adapter

issue_title: run_conversation() mutates the caller's conversation_history list
context: In Hermes, this issue is about run_conversation() mutates the caller's conversation_history list.
what_happened: Run_conversation() in run_agent.py creates an alias instead of a copy of the conversation_history parameter (line 1846): python messages = conversation_history or [] Since messages is the same object as the caller's list, every messages.append() during the conversation (user messages, assistant responses, tool calls, etc.) also modifies the caller's original list.
what_should_happen: Changed messages = conversation_history or [] to messages = list(conversation_history) if conversation_history else [] in run_conversation().
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify the behavior related to run_conversation() mutates the caller's conversation_history list in run_agent.py, tests/test_run_agent.py.
pr_title: fix(agent): copy conversation_history to avoid mutating caller's list

issue_title: /status always shows Tokens: 0 and Agent Running: No
context: In Hermes, this issue is about /status always shows Tokens: 0 and Agent Running: No.
what_happened: The /status command could incorrectly show Tokens: 0 and Agent Running: No even during an active session because live state and token counts were not being surfaced through the gateway path correctly.
what_should_happen: The status command should report the live running state and should persist and display the actual input and output token counts from the agent result.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that /status bypasses the normal queued interrupt path and reports both live agent state and persisted token totals correctly.
pr_title: fix(gateway): make /status report live state and tokens

issue_title: [Bug]: DeepSeek V3 tool call parser silently drops multi-line JSON arguments
context: In Hermes, this issue is about DeepSeek V3 tool call parser silently drops multi-line JSON arguments.
what_happened: The DeepSeek V3 tool-call parser regex did not use re.DOTALL, so multi-line JSON arguments were not captured and valid tool calls could be dropped silently.
what_should_happen: The parser should handle multi-line JSON arguments and return structured tool calls instead of falling back to raw text.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that the DeepSeek V3 parser can extract tool calls whose JSON arguments span multiple lines.
pr_title: fix: add missing re.DOTALL flag to DeepSeek V3 tool call parser

issue_title: [self-tests] test time degradation
context: In pytest, this issue is about test time degradation.
what_happened: With Pytest 8.4.0 its tests suite takes significantly more time (compared to Pytest 8.3.5).
what_should_happen: Renames Collect plugin class to PytesterHelperPlugin in pytester.inline_run() Adds gc_collect_iterations_key stash key to unraisableexception module Makes gc_collect_harder accept an iterations parameter In inline_run, sets gc_collect_iterations to 0 to avoid the slow GC collect.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that renames Collect plugin class to PytesterHelperPlugin in pytester.inline_run() Adds gc_collect_iterations_key stash key to unraisableexception module Makes gc_collect_harder accept an iterations parameter In inline_run, sets gc_collect_iterations to 0 to avoid the slow GC collect.
pr_title: pytester: avoid unraisableexception gc collects in inline runs to speed up test suite

issue_title: `KeyError` fired from `importlib` during collection when running with `--import-mode=importlib` on a directory containing a same-named one
context: In pytest, this issue is about KeyError fired from importlib during collection when running with --import-mode=importlib on a directory containing a same-named one.
what_happened: When pytest was run with --import-mode=importlib on a directory layout that contained a same-named child directory, collection could fail with a KeyError from importlib.
what_should_happen: Pytest should collect that layout cleanly under importlib mode instead of crashing during parent-package resolution.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that same-named nested-directory layouts no longer trigger the importlib KeyError during collection.
pr_title: Fixed the issue of causes KeyError when using the parameter --import-mode=importlib in pytest>=8.2 . (#12592)

issue_title: WhatsApp bridge uses Linux-only `fuser` command for orphaned process cleanup
context: In Hermes, this issue is about WhatsApp bridge uses Linux-only fuser command for orphaned process cleanup.
what_happened: WhatsAppAdapter in gateway/platforms/whatsapp.py calls the fuser command in two places to find and kill orphaned bridge processes on a given port: connect() (line ~150) - kills orphaned bridge before starting a new one disconnect() (line ~298) - kills any leftover bridge after stopping fuser is a Linux-only utility (part of psmisc package).
what_should_happen: Fuser is a Linux-only command.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify the behavior related to WhatsApp bridge uses Linux-only fuser command for orphaned process cleanup in gateway/platforms/whatsapp.py, tests/gateway/test_whatsapp_connect.py.
pr_title: fix(whatsapp): replace Linux-only fuser with cross-platform port cleanup

issue_title: Max-iterations retry summary uses hardcoded `max_tokens` instead of `_max_tokens_param()`
context: In Hermes, this issue is about Max-iterations retry summary uses hardcoded max_tokens instead of _max_tokens_param().
what_happened: In _handle_max_iterations() in run_agent.py, there are two attempts to generate a final summary when the agent hits the iteration limit: First attempt (line ~2697) - correctly uses _max_tokens_param(): python summary_kwargs.update(self._max_tokens_param(self.max_tokens)) Retry attempt (line ~2743) - hardcodes max_tokens: python summary_kwargs["max_tokens"] = self.max_tokens The _max_tokens_param() helper exists specifically because OpenAI's newer models (gpt-4o, o-series) require max_completion_tokens instead of max_tokens.
what_should_happen: The retry summary in _handle_max_iterations hardcodes max_tokens instead of calling _max_tokens_param().
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify the behavior related to Max-iterations retry summary uses hardcoded max_tokens instead of _max_tokens_param() in run_agent.py, tests/test_run_agent.py.
pr_title: fix: use _max_tokens_param in max-iterations retry path

issue_title: Context compressor can split tool-call/tool-output pairs and create dangling tool history
context: In Hermes, this issue is about Context compressor can split tool-call/tool-output pairs and create dangling tool history.
what_happened: Agent/context_compressor.py can split an assistant tool-calling turn from its contiguous tool outputs when it preserves the first/last N messages by raw message count.
what_should_happen: The fixed code should add regression coverage for compressor boundary cases around tool-call/tool-output runs verify compression does not preserve assistant tool calls without matching tool outputs verify the preserved tail never starts with orphaned tool output messages Context The underlying compressor logic was updated on main while this PR was in flight, which is why the original branch went conflicted.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify the behavior related to Context compressor can split tool-call/tool-output pairs and create dangling tool history in tests/agent/test_context_compressor.py.
pr_title: test: add regression coverage for compressor tool-call boundaries

issue_title: [Bug]: send_message advertises WhatsApp but outbound WhatsApp routing is not implemented

Problem send_message already accepts whatsapp as a platform target, but _send_to_platform(...) had no Platform.WHATSAPP branch.

issue_title: [Bug]: API Keys saving to config.yaml instead of .env as stated in doc
context: In Hermes, this issue is about API Keys saving to config.yaml instead of .env as stated in doc.
what_happened: When setting API keys it states in the docs that: "Use hermes config set to set environment variables — it automatically saves them to the right file (.env for secrets, config.yaml for everything else)." but when I tried this it saved OPEN_API_KEY to the config.yaml and not the .env as stated Steps to Reproduce hermes config set OPEN_API_KEY SK-42069-xxxxxxxxx Expected Behavior should save to .env as stated in docs Actual Behavior.

issue_title: Symlink boundary check in skills_guard uses startswith() without separator, allowing escape to sibling directories
context: In Hermes, this issue is about Symlink boundary check in skills_guard uses startswith() without separator, allowing escape to sibling directories.
what_happened: The symlink escape check in _check_structure() (tools/skills_guard.py, line 746) uses startswith() without a trailing path separator: python if not str(resolved).startswith(str(skill_dir.resolve())): This allows a symlink to escape to a sibling directory that shares a prefix.
what_should_happen: The symlink escape check in _check_structure() used startswith() without a trailing path separator.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify the behavior related to Symlink boundary check in skills_guard uses startswith() without separator, allowing escape to sibling directories in tests/tools/test_skills_guard.py, tests/tools/test_symlink_prefix_confusion.py, tools/skills_guard.py.

issue_title: remove 1-second join from threadexception plugin
context: In pytest, this issue is about remove 1-second join from threadexception plugin.
what_happened: The threadexception plugin waited up to one second for non-daemon threads to join, which added an unnecessary delay to pytest.main() even when those threads were unrelated to the tests.
what_should_happen: Pytest should not pause for that extra join delay when unrelated background threads are still running.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that pytest can finish promptly even when a long-running background thread exists.
pr_title: remove 1-second join from threadexception plugin

On fixed code (after fix): PytestRemovedIn9Warning has been removed, so the test that asserts it doesn't e.

issue_title: [Bug]: Telegram adapter missing MarkdownV2 support for strikethrough, spoiler, and blockquotes
context: In Hermes, this issue is about Telegram adapter missing MarkdownV2 support for strikethrough, spoiler, and blockquotes.
what_happened: The Telegram adapter's format_message() in gateway/platforms/telegram.py does not convert strikethrough, spoiler, or blockquote syntax to MarkdownV2.
what_should_happen: The fixed code should add strikethrough conversion (~~text~~ → ~text~) to format_message() pipeline add spoiler conversion (||text|| → ||text||) with pipe characters protected from escaping add blockquote support (> text) by preserving > at line start instead of escaping it update _strip_mdv2() to strip ~strikethrough~ and ||spoiler|| markers in plaintext fallback add 14 regression tests covering new formatting paths and edge cases Testing ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/gateway/test_telegram_format.py -q.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that format_message() correctly converts strikethrough (~~text~~), spoiler (||text||), and blockquote (> text) syntax to MarkdownV2 format instead of escaping the special characters. These tests FAIL on the buggy code and PASS on the fixed code.
pr_title: fix(telegram): add MarkdownV2 support for strikethrough, spoiler, and blockquotes

issue_title: [Bug]: Thinking type `adaptive` is not supported with `budget_tokens`
context: In Hermes, this issue is about Thinking type adaptive is not supported with budget_tokens.
what_happened: ⏱️ Time elapsed before failure: 1.13s 📝 Error: Error code: 400 - {'type': 'error', 'error': {'type': 'invalid_request_error', 'message': 'thinking.adaptive.budget_tokens: Extra inputs are not permitted'}, 'request_id': 'req_011CYzFhi93zweZCwVfmaPi When using anthropic models such opus-4-6, budget_tokens is being sent with adaptive thinking, which is not supported, either use enabled but will be deprecated or use effort instead of budget_tokens Steps to Reproduce Run hermes chat --provider anthropic --model claude-opus-4-6 Enter anything.

issue_title: Feature: Git Worktree Isolation for Parallel CLI Sessions (`hermes -w`)
context: In Hermes, this issue is about Git Worktree Isolation for Parallel CLI Sessions (hermes -w).
what_happened: When running multiple hermes-agent CLI instances concurrently on the same codebase (e.g., 10 agents fixing different issues, reviewing PRs, implementing features), they all collide in the project directory — editing each other's files, building up uncommitted changes from other tasks, getting pulled into wrong branches, and corrupting each other's git state.
what_should_happen: The fixed code should add a --worktree (-w) flag to the hermes CLI that creates an isolated git worktree for the session.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that the --worktree / -w CLI flag functionality is implemented. It FAILS on the buggy code (missing worktree support) and PASSES on the fixed code.
pr_title: feat: git worktree isolation for parallel CLI sessions (--worktree / -w)

issue_title: [Bug]: Setup wizard shows OpenRouter model list when Nous Portal model fetch fails, causing `400 missing model` errors
context: In Hermes, this issue is about Setup wizard shows OpenRouter model list when Nous Portal model fetch fails, causing 400 missing model errors.
what_happened: If fetching the Nous model list failed during setup, Hermes could silently fall back to the OpenRouter model list. Users would then save an OpenRouter-style model name that the Nous API rejected later with a 400 error.
what_should_happen: When the Nous model list is unavailable, setup should ask for a manual Nous model ID instead of falling back to the OpenRouter list.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that the Nous setup path does not fall back to OpenRouter models when the Nous fetch fails.
pr_title: fix(setup): prevent OpenRouter model list fallback for Nous provider

issue_title: pytest 8.2.0 calls `@property`s during unittest collection
context: In pytest, this issue is about pytest 8.2.0 calls @propertys during unittest collection.
what_happened: During fixture discovery, pytest would call safe_getattr(holderobj, name, None) on the instance, which would trigger @property descriptors to execute.
what_should_happen: First check the type (class) for the fixture marker without triggering descriptors, and only access the instance attribute if it's actually a fixture.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that first check the type (class) for the fixture marker without triggering descriptors, and only access the instance attribute if it's actually a fixture.
pr_title: do not discover properties when iterating fixtures

issue_title: Proposal: revisit folding skipped tests in summary
context: In pytest, this issue is about revisit folding skipped tests in summary.
what_happened: Pytest's short summary folded skipped tests together in a way that could make the output harder to read or less informative when many skip entries were present.
what_should_happen: The short test summary should present skipped tests in the intended format without losing clarity.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify the short-summary formatting behavior for skipped tests.
pr_title: feat: add --fold-skipped=yes|no cli option

issue_title: approx equal can be more restricted than `==`
context: In pytest, this issue is about approx equal can be more restricted than ==.
what_happened: This is similar to https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/13047 but is broader than that.
what_should_happen: A small documentation fix to clarify the behavior of approx.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify that the documentation clarifies that approx considers booleans unequal to numeric zero or one, which was added to address issue #13218.
pr_title: doc: clarify the behavior of approx

issue_title: search_context() / peer-card retrieval can appear empty immediately after successful session writes while dialectic recall already works
context: In Hermes, this issue is about search_context() / peer-card retrieval can appear empty immediately after successful session writes while dialectic recall already works.
what_happened: The issue title is about search results lagging behind recent session writes, but the generated test is actually reusing the .hermes.md project-config fix from a different change.
what_should_happen: For the code under test, project-level .hermes.md files should be found and included in prompt context using the repository root as the search boundary.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify the .hermes.md path: repo-root discovery, config-file discovery, frontmatter stripping, and inclusion of the config text in prompt construction.
pr_title: fix+feat: bug fixes, auto session titles, .hermes.md project config
manual_note: The generated test is misaligned with the issue and seems to target a different fix.

issue_title: [Python 3.13.0b2] `test_pdb_used_outside_test` and `test_pdb_used_in_generate_tests` are failing with Python 3.13
context: In pytest, this issue is about test_pdb_used_outside_test and test_pdb_used_in_generate_tests are failing with Python 3.13.
what_happened: Hello, test_pdb_used_outside_test and test_pdb_used_in_generate_tests are failing with Python 3.13 beta 2 on Fedora Rawhide.
what_should_happen: Python 3.13 makes pdb break on the breakpoint() call, rather than on the next line: https://docs.python.org/3/whatsnew/3.13.html#pdb Also runs the pdb tests on Python 3.13 in CI.
what_test_should_verify: The generated test is intended to verify that the pdb self-test behavior has been updated for Python 3.13, although this case is currently labeled PASS_TO_PASS in the verification results.
pr_title: Fix pdb selftests on Python 3.13

issue_title: [Bug]: Codex API model fetch uses wrong visibility filter string, leaking hidden models
context: In Hermes, this issue is about Codex API model fetch uses wrong visibility filter string, leaking hidden models.
what_happened: In hermes_cli/codex_models.py, the _fetch_models_from_api function filters hidden models with visibility == "hide" instead of "hidden".
what_should_happen: _fetch_models_from_api filters hidden Codex models with visibility == "hide", but the correct value is "hidden" (as used by _read_cache_models and confirmed by the test suite).
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify the behavior related to [Bug]: Codex API model fetch uses wrong visibility filter string, leaking hidden models in hermes_cli/codex_models.py.
pr_title: fix(cli): use correct visibility filter string in codex API model fetch

issue_title: pytest.approx considers boolean numeric types
context: In pytest, this issue is about pytest.approx considers boolean numeric types.
what_happened: From the docs: > You can also use approx to compare nonnumeric types, or dicts and sequences containing nonnumeric types, in which case it falls back to strict equality.
what_should_happen: Fixes #9353.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify the behavior related to pytest.approx considers boolean numeric types in changelog/9353.bugfix.rst, src/_pytest/python_api.py, testing/python/approx.py.
pr_title: Use exact comparison for bool in approx()

issue_title: Fixture with dependencies executes `pytest_fixture_post_finalizer` multiple times
context: In pytest, this issue is about Fixture with dependencies executes pytest_fixture_post_finalizer multiple times.
what_happened: The example below shows that the pytest_fixture_post_finalizer hook is executed n+1 times, where n is the number of fixtures depended upon.
what_should_happen: These are changes extracted from @Anton3's PR #14104.
what_test_should_verify: The generated test counts post-finalizer calls for a dependent fixture and checks that the hook is not triggered repeatedly.
pr_title: fixtures: a couple of bug fixes for pytest_fixture_post_finalizer

issue_title: Clarify rootdir relative nodeid vs cwd relative collection argument
context: In pytest, this issue is about Clarify rootdir relative nodeid vs cwd relative collection argument.
what_happened: The distinction between rootdir-relative node IDs and current-working-directory-relative collection arguments was unclear, and the related setup helper behavior could be misread or used inconsistently.
what_should_happen: Pytest should document that distinction clearly and pass the invocation directory through the relevant setup logic consistently.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify the clarified rootdir-versus-invocation-dir behavior in the setup and path-resolution code.
pr_title: Rootdir tweaks

issue_title: Security: CI/CD supply chain hardening — floating tags and unpinned actions
context: In Hermes, this issue is about Security: CI/CD supply chain hardening — floating tags and unpinned actions.
what_happened: Several CI/CD and dependency management patterns could be tightened to reduce supply chain risk.
what_should_happen: Three CI supply chain hardening changes from #3970.
what_test_should_verify: This test should verify the behavior related to Security: CI/CD supply chain hardening — floating tags and unpinned actions in .github/workflows/docs-site-checks.yml, .github/workflows/nix.yml.
pr_title: fix(ci): pin floating GitHub Actions tags and ascii-guard to explicit versions
